evals.prompt: How do I run multiple Go tasks concurrently and wait for all to finish?
evals.expected_output: Use errgroup (golang.org/x/sync/errgroup) as preferred WaitGroup + error propagation. Or sync.WaitGroup. Never start goroutines without known exit path.

evals.prompt: Should I use a buffered or unbuffered channel?
evals.expected_output: Unbuffered for strict synchronization. Buffered only for async decoupling or burst handling. Explains deadlock risk of wrong choice.

assertions.description: Distinguishes buffered vs unbuffered use cases

evals.prompt: How do I detect goroutine leaks in my Go service?
evals.expected_output: Run tests with go test -race. Use goleak library. Every goroutine must have a known exit path via context cancellation, channel close, or done signal.

assertions.description: Mentions context or done channel for lifecycle

should_not_trigger: How do I structure a Go project?
How do I handle errors in Go?
How do I write a Go HTTP server?
